data.narrative:
Ocius Technology is a Series B–stage defense and security company founded by Robert Dane. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private-sector peers at the same stage, supplemented by 2 public benchmarks (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). The private peer set includes Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, and Castelion, among others.

Peer-median valuation methodology was applied as the primary triangulation method. The median valuation across the 8 same-sector, same-stage comparables is $1.1B, with observed range of $300M to $1.5B. Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, and Harmattan AI traded at the upper end of this distribution ($1.4B–$1.5B), while Castelion anchored the midpoint at $1.0B. This method represents the sole quantitative approach available given data constraints.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $956M (low), $1.1B (median), and $1.3B (high), derived from the peer-median methodology with a ±15% adjustment band. The median figure of $1.1B reflects the central tendency of identified comparables. Key uncertainties include the materiality of Ocius's A$176M Australian Program of Record contract awarded in March 2026 relative to peers' revenue and pipeline characteristics, which are not detailed in available data. Public market comparables remain underweighted pending additional financial metrics. Revenue multiples, profitability, and contract visibility would refine this estimate materially.
